topics.recommended_public_safe_description: One-glance operating map showing how AIOS turns intent into bounded execution, evidence, review gates, public surface readiness, and business outcomes.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not imply enterprise-scale automation, production readiness, or complete system proof.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: A human-controlled work system for routing AI-assisted work through scoped execution, evidence, review, and durable records.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim autonomous operation, universal enforcement, or production readiness.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Executive orchestration layer for synthesis, sequencing, tradeoff framing, and claim-boundary review.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not replace Lyn, approve go-live, or own production readiness.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Specialist delivery workspace for engineering, QA, release critique, workflow, and implementation perspectives.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not publish unconfirmed roster names or treat Big Crew as execution-control authority.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Business and opportunity intelligence workspace for options, monetization questions, risk, and business evidence.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim POC validation, revenue proof, or active money decisions.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Worker execution support for structured runs, trace shape, fallback paths, and repeatable validation support.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim autonomous production routing or final interpretation authority.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Implementation engineer lane for bounded repo and UI changes with validation evidence.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not approve releases, define scope, or act as all of AIOS.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Flexible model access and reviewer lane when a specific provider receipt exists for the gate.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim provider superiority, cost savings, or universal reviewer authority.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Local utility lane for low-risk local support where appropriate.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not imply production model hosting, enterprise deployment, or reviewer authority.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Decision-quality review gate for evidence, scope, risk, and boundary acceptance before bounded implementation decisions.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not replace Lyn, approve go-live, or certify production readiness.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Coordination role that keeps scope, Definition of Done, evidence package, and owner gate clear.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not make final risk decisions or approve release.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Role-based review pattern for source mapping, claim boundary, release verification, leakage, workflow friction, and scope checks.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not present as independent autonomous multi-worker proof unless separately evidenced.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Evidence and claim-boundary checker that finds gaps, source mismatches, and unsafe claims.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not replace QA, owner review, production readiness, or public proof approval.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Bounded task executor for approved scoped work.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not create scope, self-approve, or prove performance impact.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Execution-control role for task boundary, authority, dependencies, caveats, and stop conditions.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not act as an autonomous platform or approve production readiness.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Deterministic local validation step that packages public-surface changes for owner review.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not deploy, approve go-live, certify production readiness, or replace owner semantic acceptance.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Release lane that helps package recurring public-page updates for owner review before go-live consideration.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No auto-deploy, no go-live approval, no production authority, no full QA claim.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Evidence-structure and dashboard-readiness support lane that reviews data/dashboard ambiguity before escalation.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not treat as final decision authority, production data platform, or proof of live analytics.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Snapshot-based readiness and missingness surface that explains which decisions remain blocked.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No benchmark proof, provider superiority, cost saving, routing correctness, or live observability claim.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Rules that separate public-safe claims from draft, local, incomplete, or evidence-missing work.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not let role labels, dashboards, or achievements become proof without evidence.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Risk that public pages, source records, and evidence artifacts drift unless a canonical map controls placement.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not expose private drift records or imply full prevention.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Static public-safe visibility into status, blocked actions, and evidence boundaries.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No live telemetry, uptime, latency, or production monitoring claim.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Distinguishes informational signals from checks that block claims, release wording, or next actions.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not treat advisory metrics as proof or approval.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Clear split between recommending, executing, checking, approving, and owning risk.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not collapse worker, checker, reviewer, and owner authority into one label.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Local repeatable checks that produce PASS/WARN/BLOCK/NOT_APPLICABLE evidence for bounded claims.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Does not guarantee correctness or replace semantic owner review.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: When a public-surface or source-state inconsistency is found, diagnose the cause before broad patching.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim all future drift is prevented.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Separates local validation, preview/deploy action, live verification, and production-readiness claims.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not claim deployment equals production readiness, full QA, or go-live approval.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Protected decisions stay with Lyn; AIOS roles can recommend, check, block, or escalate depending on scope.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No delegated role owns money, privacy, legal, reputation, health, career, public release, or major architecture decisions.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Durable truth lives in reviewed knowledge records and Git; public pages are presentation surfaces.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Do not let dashboards, chat memory, or page appearance override source records.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Rules and review lanes that keep public wording aligned to evidence, source-of-truth, and owner review.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No auto-publish, no automatic semantic acceptance, no private evidence exposure.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Static public-safe review of signals, records, checks, and limitations.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No realtime tracking, uptime monitoring, production observability, or analytics validation claim.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Bounded validation evidence for authority semantics, human gates, downgrade behavior, and sanitizer behavior.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No universal enforcement, production gate operation, or live monitoring claim.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Governed release discipline that uses scope, validation, public-safety checks, and owner gates before public changes are treated as accepted.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: No full release automation, no auto-go-live, no guaranteed prevention.

topics.recommended_public_safe_description: Public proof updates that summarize bounded achievements with caveats, evidence references, and blocked claims.
forbidden_unsafe_claims: Achievements are not canonical policy, production readiness, or full proof unless separate evidence supports that.

future_update_rules: Any new role, team, lane, or gate must have exactly one canonical public page and a bounded set of supporting pages.
Any public claim must map to evidence, claim boundary, and a route that owns the public wording.
Any role with authority must declare what it can recommend, block, approve, or escalate.
Runner, checker, and gate wording must not imply deployment, go-live, or production-readiness authority unless explicitly authorized.
Deployment wording must distinguish local validation, preview/deploy action, live verification, and production-readiness claims.
Cockpit/status pages do not become source of truth by looking official.
Architecture and org-chart updates should use grouped lanes before adding many individual public cards.
Any topic added to one public page must be checked against this topic map for consistency.
